summary: A summary of Martin Heidegger's 1927 magnum opus, Being and Time, which attempts to revive ontology through the analysis of Dasein (being-in-the-world). The text outlines his rejection of Cartesian dualism, his views on time and mortality, and his methodological use of phenomenology, hermeneutics, and destructuring.

principles.statement: Subject and object are inseparable in human existence.
principles.rationale: Human beings are not detached subjective spectators of objects, but are fundamentally engaged in the world as a unitary phenomenon.
principles.source_quote: The book instead holds that both subject and object are inseparable.

principles.statement: Time is understood only from a finite or mortal vantage.
principles.rationale: Time finds its meaning in death, and human existence is temporally stretched between birth and death.
principles.source_quote: Heidegger believes that time finds its meaning in death

principles.rationale: Consciousness cannot be the determinant of existence; rather, it is merely an effect of Dasein's fundamental being-in-the-world.
principles.source_quote: Consciousness is thus an "effect" rather than a determinant of existence.

principles.rationale: Mundane being-in-the-world provides access to the meaning of being, which elucidates prescientific understanding before logic or theory can be applied.
principles.source_quote: This meaning would then elucidate ordinary "prescientific" understanding, which precedes abstract ways of knowing, such as logic or theory.

frameworks.name: Hermeneutic Circle
frameworks.when_to_apply: When seeking to increase the depth of engagement and understanding of an experience or text.
steps: Move from the parts of experience to the whole of experience.
Move back and forth between the parts and the whole repeatedly.
Continue spiraling until reaching a place of sensible meaning, free of inner contradictions.
frameworks.source_quote: moves from the parts of experience to the whole of experience and back and forth again and again to increase the depth of engagement and understanding.

frameworks.name: Destructuring (Destruktion)
frameworks.when_to_apply: When analyzing the philosophical tradition to uncover hidden fundamental experiences of being.
steps: Examine theoretical knowledge and the metaphysics of presence.
Refute the incorrect assumption that theoretical knowledge is fundamental to being.
Reveal the fundamental experience of being hidden within the theoretical attitude.
frameworks.source_quote: an interpretative strategy that reveals the fundamental experience of being hidden within the theoretical attitude of the metaphysics of presence.

mental_models.name: Dasein (Being-in-the-world)
mental_models.description: A lens that views human beings not as isolated subjects, but as unitary, living beings fundamentally intertwined with their objective world and activities.
mental_models.example: Understanding a person not as a spectator looking at a world, but as an entity defined by its active "being there" and engagement with its surroundings.

mental_models.name: Thrownness and Historicality
mental_models.description: The concept that individuals are "thrown" into a world with a pre-existing history and tradition, which dictates the future possibilities they are charged with assuming.
mental_models.example: Recognizing that one's access to the world and future goals is always mediated by the "pastness" of their cultural and historical tradition.

signature_quotes.text: 'Being' is not something like a being but is rather "what determines beings as beings."
signature_quotes.context: Defining his central concept of Being.

signature_quotes.text: in terms of which something becomes intelligible as something.
signature_quotes.context: Describing how Dasein provides access to the meaning of being.

anti_patterns.description: Viewing the human being as a subjective spectator of objects (Cartesian dualism).
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It treats subject and object as separate, additive combinations rather than recognizing them as the inseparable, unitary phenomenon of "being-in-the-world."

anti_patterns.description: Believing that theoretical knowledge is fundamental to being.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Abstract ways of knowing, such as logic or theory, are preceded by ordinary "prescientific" understanding; theory hides the fundamental experience of being.
Consciousness is peripheral to Dasein and is merely an "effect" rather than a determinant of existence.
